name: Nexus
what: Autonomous agent platform (product 'Cue') paired with forward-deployed engineers; agents complete end-to-end business workflows across 4,000+ enterprise systems with full read/write and deploy into the channels teams already use (Slack, Teams, WhatsApp, email, phone, web). Positions explicitly as 'AI that completes work, not answers questions'. Customers incl. Orange Group, Lambda.
why_tier: Torn T2/T3 -> higher per hard rule 4. Execution-first horizontal agent platform (the opposite wedge from Astell's intelligence/open-loops), but its huge cross-tool read/write reach + in-channel deployment put it one positioning pivot ('surface cross-tool intelligence') from the lane. Not single-feature/pure-infra, so Tier-3's buckets don't fit; scored T2 to err against a miss.
notes: Nexus Enterprises, Inc.; $4.3M seed (announced 2026; 'Cue' shipped). Verified via own-domain pages agent.nexus/blog/dust-alternatives + the seed announcement. Founders/HQ/founding year not surfaced; customer base (Orange + European telecoms) suggests an EU footprint. Execution-first - flag for trajectory toward intelligence/answers.

name: Tacnode
what: 'Context Lake' — distributed, PostgreSQL-compatible context infrastructure that keeps AI agents and services operating on the same shared, live, and semantic context (sub-second freshness, strong consistency, temporal versioning). Positioned as a feature-store / vector-DB / lakehouse alternative delivering 'decision coherence'; workloads = shared multi-agent memory, live feature serving, decision-time analytics. In production at a large food-delivery platform; on AWS Marketplace; SOC 2 Type II + HIPAA.
why_tier: Infra selling Astell's shared-context-store pillar a la carte — 'a shared context layer where all decision-makers incl. stateful AI agents operate on the same reality.' Same infra cluster as Cognee/Zep/Mem0/xmemory (Tier 2 / infra). Center of gravity is data-plumbing (a real-time DB for agents), not an application-layer answers/intelligence rival, so T2 not T1; one positioning step from the lane.
notes: Emerged from stealth Jan 2026 (Tacnode Context Lake). Domain is tacnode.io (the 2026-06-15 memory note's 'tacnode.com' was wrong). Built on PostgreSQL wire protocol + Apache Iceberg; separation of compute/storage. Launch/press: https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/01/20/3221772/0/en/Tacnode-Emerges-from-Stealth-to-Launch-Tacnode-Context-Lake-a-Shared-Context-Layer-for-AI-Agents.html . Funding amount/stage + HQ city not disclosed in surfaced sources; founding ~2024-25 (copyright '2025 Tacnode Inc.'). Prior-miss recovery from the 2026-06-15 dual-run.

name: Zaro
what: AI-native workspace that connects a company's enterprise data, workflows and AI tools into a single shared context layer that retains and reuses organizational knowledge; that context then powers custom apps built from the company's own files and decisions. Replaces a sprawl of separate AI tools with one company-owned workspace; routes routine tasks to cheaper models (claims ~10x cost cut vs frontier-only).
why_tier: Shared cross-tool context layer retaining/reusing organizational knowledge across enterprise data + workflows + tools = Astell's exact promise (pillars 1-2), with a 'company-owned, take-your-context-with-you' independence wedge. Direct lane; a prospect would choose between this and Astell -> Tier 1 / direct (rule 4: higher when torn vs. the agent-workspace framing).
notes: Out of stealth 2026-06-09; $5.1M pre-seed led by Cherry Ventures, angels incl. Thomas Wolf (Hugging Face co-founder), Thomas Dohmke (GitHub CEO), Charlie Songhurst, Mandeep Singh, and Convergence co-founders Marvin Purtorab + Andy Toulis. 8-person team; 5 ex-Convergence (acquired by Salesforce; shipped Agentforce). Cherry's own post: https://cherry.vc/news/shared-memory-for-ai-agents . Prior-miss recovery: caught by the 2026-06-15 Thursday bridge but missed by that day's merged run. Founding year not disclosed.
